name: Quadrifilar Helix Antenna
description: A four-wire helical antenna wound in a helical configuration with 90-degree phase differences between adjacent elements. Provides excellent circular polarization characteristics and hemispherical coverage patterns. Commonly used for satellite communications and GPS applications due to its compact size and broad beamwidth.

helix_length.name: Helix Length
helix_length.description: Total axial length of the helix

pitch_angle.name: Pitch Angle
pitch_angle.description: Angle between helix wire and ground plane

ground_plane_diameter.name: Ground Plane Diameter
ground_plane_diameter.description: Diameter of the ground plane reflector

number_of_turns.name: Number of Turns
number_of_turns.description: Total number of helical turns per element

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves determining helix geometry for desired frequency, calculating feed network for quadrature phasing, optimizing for circular polarization purity, and matching to desired impedance.

steps.title: Determine Operating Frequency and Wavelength
steps.description: Calculate free-space wavelength and establish design frequency

steps.title: Calculate Helix Diameter
steps.description: Set helix diameter for optimal radiation characteristics

steps.title: Determine Helix Length and Pitch
steps.description: Calculate axial length and pitch angle for quarter-wave resonance

steps.title: Design Feed Network
steps.description: Create 90-degree hybrid network for quadrature feeding

steps.title: Calculate Input Impedance
steps.description: Determine individual element impedance and feed network matching

steps.title: Optimize Ground Plane Size
steps.description: Size ground plane for desired front-to-back ratio

steps.title: Verify Circular Polarization
steps.description: Check axial ratio and adjust geometry if needed

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use transmission line model with helical geometry corrections and mutual coupling between elements
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Apply superposition of four helical elements with 90-degree phase progression and pattern multiplication
keyAssumptions: Negligible mutual coupling between non-adjacent elements

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 9
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 8
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 7
Kraus, J.D. and Marhefka, R.J., 'Antennas for All Applications', 3rd Edition
